title: asplenium scolopendrium var. antri-jovis
intro: Asplenium scolopendrium var. antri-jovis, also known as the Hart's-tongue fern, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Hart's-tongue fern, a species of fern with a long, tongue-shaped frond. The medicinal properties of this plant are mainly attributed to its ability to act as a mild purgative, helping to stimulate bowel movements and alleviate constipation. The health benefits of Asplenium scolopendrium var. antri-jovis are thought to include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as a mild diuretic, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, but caution should be exercised due to its potential to cause stomach upset and allergic reactions in some individuals.
